sequences.mixStrategy: This ambient/atmospheric energize arrangement uses four pad layers that build from a gentle wash into a shimmering, driving wall of sound, then recede gracefully. FREQUENCY SEPARATION: The Warm Pad bass (C2-C3, 20-300Hz) provides the sub-foundation with long droning roots that anchor every harmonic shift. The Choir Pad chords (C3-C5, 300Hz-2kHz) fill the harmonic body with open sus2/maj7 voicings — root-5th-9th spacing to keep them airy yet present. The New Age Pad melody (C4-C6, 1-8kHz) floats above with shimmering melodic phrases built from chord tones on strong beats and Lydian passing tones (#4, maj7) on weak beats. The Sweep Pad texture (C5-G5, 3kHz+) adds crystalline shimmer and evolving high-frequency air. HARMONIC UNITY: All tracks follow Csus2 → Fmaj7 → Cmaj7 → Am simultaneously. In C Lydian: C-D-E-F#-G-A-B. The sus2 chords use C-D-G, Fmaj7 uses F-A-C-E, Cmaj7 uses C-E-G-B, Am uses A-C-E. Chord changes happen every ~11.25 seconds (segments of the 45s piece). ENERGY ARC: Segments 0-7.5s (0.4 energy) — sparse, few voices, low velocity. 7.5-15s (0.6) — more notes enter, chords thicken. 15-22.5s (0.8) — melody becomes more active, bass pulses subtly. 22.5-30s (0.9) — full density, highest velocities, all layers active. 30-37.5s (1.0) — peak energy, fastest melodic movement, richest chords. 37.5-45s (0.7) — graceful descent, thinning out, velocities drop. RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Despite ambient genre, the ENERGIZE mode means notes have shorter sustains (1-4s for melody, 2-6s for chords) creating a pulsing, breathing quality rather than static drones. Bass provides slow rhythmic anchoring while melody creates forward motion with overlapping phrases. All tracks breathe together — when melody rests, texture fills; when chords shift, bass moves first as a signal.

This ambient focus score uses four pad layers occupying distinct frequency bands to create a warm, enveloping sonic field without rhythmic pulse. FREQUENCY SEPARATION: The Warm Pad bass (C2-C3, 20-300Hz) provides the gravitational foundation with long drone notes on chord roots, shifting every 8-12 seconds. The Choir Pad chords (C3-C5, 300Hz-2kHz) fill the harmonic body with open sus2/maj7 voicings using root-5th-9th spacing, creating spacious mid-range warmth. The New Age Pad melody (C4-C6, 1-8kHz) floats above with gentle, sparse melodic fragments using chord tones on entries and Lydian scale passing tones, creating the most prominent but still gentle voice. The Sweep Pad texture (C5-G5, 3kHz+) adds high shimmer with extremely long, quiet notes that evolve slowly. HARMONIC COHESION: All tracks follow the same slow modal progression — Csus2 (0-11s) → Fmaj7 (11-22s) → Cmaj7 (22-33s) → Am (33-45s). Bass plays roots C, F, C, A. Chords voice each chord openly. Melody uses chord tones as anchor points. ENERGY ARC: Starts sparse and quiet (0.4), gradually adds density and slightly higher velocities through the middle (0.6→0.7), sustains at 0.7 through the core, then gently recedes (0.5) at the end. Notes overlap freely with long sustains (2-12s) creating gentle interference patterns. No rhythmic grid — entries are staggered at non-quantized times to feel organic and drifting. The result should habituate quickly for focus, providing a steady, predictable harmonic bath that the listener can sink into without distraction.

This ambient/atmospheric relaxation score in D Lydian is built as a unified, breathing organism across four frequency layers. FREQUENCY SEPARATION: The Warm Pad bass (MIDI 38-50, 20-300Hz) provides the gravitational foundation with long drone roots; the Choir Pad chords (MIDI 54-69, 300Hz-2kHz) fill the harmonic body with open sus2/maj7 voicings spaced widely; the New Age Pad melody (MIDI 66-81, 1-8kHz) floats above with sparse, singing tones that trace chord tones on downbeats; and the Sweep Pad texture (MIDI 72-88, 3kHz+) adds crystalline shimmer in the highest register. HARMONIC COHESION: All four tracks follow the same slow chord progression — Dsus2 (0-11s) → Gmaj7 (11-22s) → Dmaj7 (22-33s) → Bm (33-45s). Every chord change happens simultaneously across all tracks. Bass always plays roots (D, G, D, B). Chords voice the full harmony. Melody plays chord tones on strong entries and Lydian passing tones between. ENERGY ARC: Segments roughly divide into 7.5s blocks. Energy starts sparse and quiet (vel 35-45), builds gently through the middle (vel 50-65), peaks at segment 4, then recedes to a whisper. The piece breathes — no rhythmic pulse, just overlapping sustains that drift freely. Notes enter at irregular intervals to avoid mechanical feel. RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Bass notes sustain 8-12s, chords overlap in 4-6s waves, melody notes last 3-6s with gentle gaps, and texture sparkles appear every 3-5s with long 5-8s tails. The staggered entries create a shimmering, cloud-like effect where no two tracks attack simultaneously, giving each voice space to emerge.

This ambient sleep score is built around the progression Fsus2 → Bbmaj7 → Fmaj7 → Dm, moving in slow modal shifts across 45 seconds (~6 segments of ~7.5s each). FREQUENCY SEPARATION: The Warm Pad bass (GM 89) anchors the sub-bass/bass range (MIDI 29-43, ~50-200Hz) with long 8-16s drone roots, providing a dark, warm foundation. The Choir Pad chords (GM 91) fill the low-mid to mid range (MIDI 48-67, ~300Hz-1.5kHz) with open sus2/maj7 voicings (root-5th-9th spacing), creating harmonic body without clutter. The New Age Pad melody (GM 88) floats in the high-mid/presence range (MIDI 65-79, ~1-4kHz) with sparse, singing phrases of 4-8s sustains that trace chord tones on strong beats and Lydian scale passing tones between. The Sweep Pad texture (GM 95) shimmers in the upper presence range (MIDI 72-84, ~3-8kHz) with ultra-sparse, ghostly long tones adding air and dimension. ENERGY ARC: All tracks breathe together — starting very sparse and quiet (vel 25-35), gently swelling to modest warmth mid-piece (vel 35-50), then fading back to near-silence. Bass and chords change on the same beats; melody anticipates or echoes chord changes with approach tones. No rhythmic pulse exists — notes drift in with staggered entries creating gentle overlaps. The overall effect is a dark, enveloping cloud of sound where each layer occupies its own sonic space: bass = earth, chords = body, melody = breath, texture = sky.

Driving 12-bar blues energizer: Steel Guitar leads with bluesy bends and phrases in mid-range, Electric Bass walks quarter-note lines through chord tones and chromatic approaches, Piano comps with rhythmic 7th-chord stabs on upbeats, Drums provide shuffle feel with triplet hi-hats. Mix: Drums foundation, Bass anchoring low-end, Piano mid-spread, Steel Guitar on top. All follow A7(I7)-D7(IV7)-E7(V7) 12-bar form.

sequences.mixStrategy: This is a slow-burn blues focus track in E at 75 BPM with shuffle feel. Quarter note = 0.800s. The 12-bar blues form (I7=E7, IV7=A7, V7=B7) cycles over ~28.8s, giving us roughly 1.5 cycles in 45s. FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Electric Bass, MIDI 28-47) owns the sub-bass and bass range (20-300Hz), locking with the kick drum on beats 1 and 3 while walking through chord tones and chromatic approaches. Piano comping (MIDI 52-67) fills the low-mid to mid range (300Hz-2kHz), voicing dominant 7th chords on beats 2 and 4 in a shuffle comp pattern, staying out of the bass and melody ranges. Steel Guitar melody (MIDI 60-76) sits in the high-mid/presence range (1-5kHz), playing languid blues licks with space between phrases — call-and-response style — emphasizing chord tones on strong beats and blue notes (Bb/b5) as passing tones. Drums provide the rhythmic skeleton: kick on 1&3, snare on 2&4, with a shuffle hi-hat pattern using triplet subdivisions. RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Bass and kick lock together on beats 1 and 3. Snare and piano comp lock on beats 2 and 4. Melody floats over the top, breathing in the spaces. ENERGY ARC: We start gentle (0.4) for bars 1-2, build to 0.6 for bars 3-6, reach a plateau of 0.7 for bars 7-12, sustain through the second cycle bars 1-8, then taper to 0.5 for the final bars. Velocities stay in the focus-mode range (45-75 mostly), with melody peaking at 72 on climactic phrases. The overall effect is a warm, steady, hypnotic blues groove that habituates quickly — perfect for deep focus.

THREE-TRACK BLUES RELAXATION MIX STRATEGY:

FREQUENCY SEPARATION:
- Electric Bass (100-300Hz): Provides the warm, deep foundation with a relaxed walking bass feel. Stays in MIDI 33-48 range (A1-C3). Root motion anchors each chord change, with chromatic approach tones and 5ths as passing tones. Slow, deliberate quarter-note walking with occasional half-notes for breathing space.
- Piano Comping (300Hz-2kHz): Fills the harmonic mid-range with dominant 7th voicings in MIDI 48-67 range (C3-G4). Comps on beats 2 and 4 in shuffle style, leaving space for melody. Voiced with 3rds and 7ths prominent for that blues color. Gentle velocity (40-60) for relaxation warmth.
- Steel Guitar Melody (1kHz-8kHz): Floats above in MIDI 60-79 range (C4-G5). Plays lyrical blues licks with long sustains (2-6s), grace-note bends (chromatic approach from below), and call-and-response phrasing. Velocity 45-65 for gentle expressiveness.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK:
- Bass walks steady quarter/half notes, providing the pulse
- Piano comps on beats 2&4, locking with bass on chord changes
- Melody plays between piano hits, breathing in the spaces, with long sustained blue notes

HARMONIC UNITY (12-bar blues in A at 61 BPM, ~0.984s per beat, ~3.93s per bar):
- Bars 1-4 (0.0-15.7s): A7 — Energy 0.3→0.5, gentle opening
- Bars 5-6 (15.7-23.6s): D7 — Energy 0.5→0.6, warming up
- Bars 7-8 (23.6-31.5s): A7 — Energy 0.7, emotional peak
- Bar 9 (31.5-35.4s): E7 — Energy 0.7→0.6, turnaround begins
- Bar 10 (35.4-39.3s): D7 — Energy 0.6→0.4, winding down
- Bars 11-12 (39.3-45.0s): A7 — Energy 0.4, gentle resolution

ENERGY ARC: All three tracks follow the same arc — sparse/quiet at start, building together to bar 7-9 peak, then gently fading. Bass gets slightly more active, piano voicings get fuller, melody gets more expressive at the peak, then all relax together.

SLEEP BLUES MIX STRATEGY — Key of E, 48 BPM, 45 seconds

FREQUENCY SEPARATION:
• Bass (Electric Bass, 20-300Hz, MIDI 28-44): Ultra-slow, warm whole notes and half notes on chord roots. Sits in the sub-bass/bass region providing a dark, enveloping foundation. Minimal movement — mostly roots with occasional 5ths as gentle passing tones. Velocity 25-45 for sleep mode.
• Chords (Piano, 300Hz-2kHz, MIDI 48-67): Sparse, sustained dominant 7th voicings. Soft comping — one chord every 2-4 bars, letting each voicing ring out and decay naturally. Ghost-like presence filling the harmonic midrange. Velocity 25-40.
• Melody (Steel Guitar, 1kHz-8kHz, MIDI 60-76): Extremely sparse, slow blues licks with long sustains (4-12s). Mimics a sleepy steel guitar crying softly in the night. Blue notes (Bb, G) used for that aching, drowsy quality. Velocity 25-50. Lots of space between phrases.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK:
No drums in sleep mode. All three tracks breathe together in a slow, free-floating pulse. Bass provides the harmonic anchor with very long tones. Piano chords appear gently between bass movements. Melody floats above with long, singing phrases that resolve on chord tones. The feel is like a lullaby played by a blues trio at 3 AM, barely awake.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments, ~7.5s each):
0.0-7.5s (0.3): Whisper entrance — bass root only, single piano chord, melody enters with one gentle note
7.5-15.0s (0.4): Slightly more movement — bass walks gently, piano adds another voicing, melody offers a short lick
15.0-22.5s (0.5): Peak warmth — all three voices present, melody has its most expressive (but still gentle) phrase
22.5-30.0s (0.5): Sustained warmth — V7-IV7 turnaround, gentle tension and release
30.0-37.5s (0.4): Settling down — fewer notes, longer sustains, melody fading
37.5-45.0s (0.3): Dissolution — bass holds final root, piano whispers last chord, melody trails off

CHORD PROGRESSION (12-bar blues in E at 48 BPM, quarter = 1.25s, bar = 5.0s, 12 bars ≈ 60s — compressed to ~9 bars for 45s):
Adapted 12-bar for 45s (9 bars × 5s):
Bar 1-2 (0-10s): E7
Bar 3-4 (10-20s): A7 (2 bars) then E7 (brief)
Bar 5-6 (20-30s): E7 → B7
Bar 7 (30-35s): A7
Bar 8-9 (35-45s): E7 (resolution)

All tracks change chords TOGETHER on the same beats. Bass always plays the root. Piano voicings shift to match. Melody resolves to chord tones on downbeats.

This Classical energize arrangement in D major is built around a driving, rhythmic energy that builds across 6 segments (~7.5s each). FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Cello, 20-300Hz, MIDI 38-50) anchors the harmonic foundation with strong root notes and Alberti-style movement. Chords (String Ensemble, 300Hz-2kHz, MIDI 50-69) provide sustained harmonic body with close voicings that change precisely with the progression. Melody (Acoustic Piano, 1-8kHz, MIDI 62-81) delivers a lyrical, singable theme with antecedent-consequent phrasing, chord tones on strong beats. Texture (Flute, 3kHz+, MIDI 74-88) adds shimmering counter-melody ornaments in the highest register. HARMONIC PLAN: Segments 1-2 (0-15s): I→IV→V→I progression establishing the key. Segments 3-4 (15-30s): I→vi→IV→V progression adding emotional depth and building intensity. Segments 5-6 (30-45s): Return to I→IV→V→I with climactic energy, then settling. ENERGY ARC: 0.4→0.6→0.8→0.9→1.0→0.7. Velocity and note density increase through seg 5, then ease in seg 6. RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Cello bass locks with strong beats, piano melody breathes between phrases, strings sustain through changes, flute ornaments fill gaps in melody. All tracks change chords on the same beats for total cohesion. Quarter note = 0.522s. Each chord lasts ~2 beats (1.044s) or 4 beats (2.088s) depending on harmonic rhythm.

This Classical focus score in D Major at 75 BPM is built around a clear harmonic foundation with four complementary tracks occupying distinct frequency bands. The CELLO BASS (20-300Hz) anchors the harmony with sustained roots and fifths in the C2-C4 range, providing a warm, steady foundation using legato whole and half notes. The STRING ENSEMBLE CHORDS (300Hz-2kHz) fill the harmonic body in the C3-C5 range with close-voiced triads and seventh chords, sustaining through each harmony change with gentle swells. The PIANO MELODY (1-8kHz) carries a lyrical, singable theme in the C4-C6 range with balanced 2-bar antecedent-consequent phrases, emphasizing chord tones on strong beats with stepwise passing tones on weak beats. The FLUTE TEXTURE (3kHz+) adds shimmer and air in the C5-C7 range with sparse counter-melodic fragments that ornament the melody without competing. All four tracks lock to the same chord progression: Segment 1-2 (0-16s): I(D) → IV(G) → V(A) → I(D); Segment 3-6 (16-45s): I(D) → vi(Bm) → IV(G) → V(A), repeated with variation. Energy arc: bars 1-2 at 0.4 (gentle entry), bars 3-4 at 0.6, bars 5-8 at 0.7 (sustained plateau), bars 9-10 at 0.7, final bars easing to 0.5. Velocity stays in 45-75 range for focus-mode habituation. Rhythmic interlock: bass plays on beats 1 and 3, chords sustain across bars, melody weaves eighth-note and quarter-note motion between bass pulses, flute enters on off-beats with gentle ornaments. All chord changes are synchronized across every track.

This arrangement is a gentle Classical relaxation piece in G major, designed so each track occupies a distinct frequency band for a warm, spacious sound. BASS (Cello, 20-300Hz, MIDI 36-55): Provides the harmonic foundation with sustained roots and occasional 5ths, anchoring every chord change. Plays long legato notes in the C2-C4 range. CHORDS (String Ensemble, 300Hz-2kHz, MIDI 48-67): Fills the harmonic body with close-voiced triads and 7ths, sustaining smooth pads that shift with proper voice leading—common tones are held across chord changes. MELODY (Acoustic Piano, 1-8kHz, MIDI 60-79): The lead voice, playing a lyrical singable theme with antecedent-consequent phrasing. Chord tones land on strong beats, with stepwise passing tones on weak beats. TEXTURE (Flute, 3kHz+, MIDI 72-88): A sparse, ornamental counter-melody that floats above everything, adding shimmer and air with gentle phrases that breathe between the melody's statements. ENERGY ARC: Segments (~7.5s each): 0.3→0.5→0.6→0.7→0.6→0.4. The piece begins softly with sparse notes and low velocities, gradually builds to a gentle climax around 22-30s, then gracefully recedes. All tracks follow the same chord progression: I(G)→IV(C)→V(D)→I(G) for the first half, then I(G)→vi(Em)→IV(C)→V(D) for the second half. Bass always plays chord roots. Chord changes happen simultaneously across all tracks. Rhythmic interlock: bass sustains under chords, melody floats above with longer phrases, texture adds gentle filigree in the gaps.

This sleep-mode Classical arrangement is built around a gentle I→IV→V→I then I→vi→IV→V progression in C major, with each track occupying a distinct frequency band to create a warm, enveloping sonic blanket.

FREQUENCY SEPARATION:
- BASS (Cello, 20-300Hz, MIDI 36-48): Provides the harmonic foundation with sustained root notes and occasional 5ths. Very long notes (4-8s) anchoring each chord change. Sits in the sub-bass/bass region.
- CHORDS (String Ensemble, 300Hz-2kHz, MIDI 48-67): Gentle sustained triads and 7th chords in close voicing. Provides the harmonic body with smooth voice leading — common tones are held across chord changes. Warm, pillowy pad.
- MELODY (Acoustic Piano, 1-8kHz, MIDI 60-76): A lyrical, stepwise lullaby theme with long note values. Plays chord tones on strong beats, with gentle scalar passing tones. Very sparse — breathing space between phrases.
- TEXTURE (Flute, 3kHz+, MIDI 72-84): Ethereal counter-melody fragments, extremely sparse. Provides shimmer and air. Responds to the piano melody in call-and-response fashion.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments of ~7.5s each):
0-7.5s: 0.3 — Only bass and soft chords enter. Piano plays first note at end.
7.5-15s: 0.4 — Piano melody enters gently, strings swell slightly.
15-22.5s: 0.5 — Peak warmth. Flute enters. All four voices present.
22.5-30s: 0.5 — Sustained fullness, second phrase of progression.
30-37.5s: 0.4 — Gradual retreat. Flute thins out, velocities drop.
37.5-45s: 0.3 — Final plagal/authentic cadence. Everything fades to near-silence.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: At 48 BPM, a quarter note = 1.25s. All tracks change chords together. The bass sustains through chord changes, the strings shift voicings smoothly, the piano places notes on beats 1 and 3, and the flute floats above with ornamental fragments offset from the piano. No drums — pure tonal warmth for sleep.

CHORD TIMING (each chord ~5.625s = ~4.5 beats at 48BPM, 8 chords across 45s):
C (0-5.6) → F (5.6-11.25) → G (11.25-16.9) → C (16.9-22.5) → C (22.5-28.1) → Am (28.1-33.75) → F (33.75-39.4) → G (39.4-45)

FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Synth Bass, MIDI 33-45) anchors the sub-bass and bass region (20-300Hz) with pumping root-octave patterns locked to the kick drum. Chords (Warm Pad, MIDI 48-67) fill the low-mid to mid range (300Hz-2kHz) with sustained Am, Dm, F, and G minor chord voicings providing harmonic body. Melody (Sawtooth Lead, MIDI 64-79) occupies high-mid to presence range (1-8kHz) with arpeggiated 16th-note synth patterns that weave around chord tones. Drums provide full-spectrum rhythmic backbone with four-on-the-floor kick, claps on 2&4, and hi-hat patterns.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Kick and bass lock together on every beat with the bass doing root-octave pumps on 8th notes. Hi-hats fill the 8th-note grid. Melody 16th-note arpeggios breathe between kick hits, creating forward momentum. Chords sustain underneath providing a bed.

HARMONIC UNITY: All tracks follow the same 4-bar progression: Am (i) → Am (i) → F (bVI) → G (bVII), repeating. Every chord change happens simultaneously across all tracks. Bass plays roots A and octave bounces, then F roots, then G roots. Chords voice full triads. Melody targets chord tones on strong beats.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments, ~7.5s each): Seg1 (0-7.5s, 0.4): Drums + bass only, sparse. Seg2 (7.5-15s, 0.6): Chords enter, bass fuller. Seg3 (15-22.5s, 0.8): Melody enters with arpeggios, energy builds. Seg4 (22.5-30s, 0.9): Full density, melody more active. Seg5 (30-37.5s, 1.0): Peak energy, all tracks at max density/velocity. Seg6 (37.5-45s, 0.7): Breakdown, melody thins, energy drops for resolution.

This Electronic/House focus track is built around a steady i→bVI→bVII→i progression in Am (Am→F→G→Am), cycling every 6.4 seconds (8 beats at 75 BPM). The mix is carefully stratified: BASS (Synth Bass, 20-300Hz) anchors the low end with a pumping root-octave pattern on A1/A2, F1/F2, G1/G2, locking tightly to the kick drum on every beat. CHORDS (Warm Pad, 300Hz-2kHz) fill the harmonic body with sustained minor/major voicings in the C3-C5 range, providing a lush bed that moves slowly, creating a hypnotic wash. MELODY (Sawtooth Lead, 1-8kHz) sits above in C4-C6, playing arpeggiated 16th-note synth patterns that outline chord tones on strong beats with scalar passing tones between. DRUMS (full spectrum, rhythmic) provide the four-on-floor pulse: kick every beat (95 vel), clap on 2&4 (80 vel), closed hi-hats on 8ths (45-60 vel). Energy arc: segments 1-2 (0-15s) build from 0.4→0.6 with bass+drums introducing, then chords entering; segments 3-5 (15-37.5s) sustain 0.7 energy with all elements active and melody fully present; segment 6 (37.5-45s) drops to 0.5 with melody thinning and velocity reducing. Rhythmic interlock: bass hits lock with kick on downbeats, melody patterns breathe on the offbeats between hi-hat hits. All tracks change chords together at the same beat boundaries. The result is a steady, predictable, habituating electronic pulse ideal for focus.

This relaxation electronic/house arrangement in Cm uses a gentle, spacious approach with three tracks occupying distinct frequency bands. BASS (Synth Bass, 20-300Hz): Provides the low-end foundation with slow, pumping root-octave patterns on C2/G2 following the chord progression i→bVI→bVII→i (Cm→Ab→Bb→Cm). The bass uses long sustains and gentle velocity (40-60) to create a warm, pillowy low end rather than aggressive pumping. It locks to half-note and whole-note rhythms. CHORDS (Warm Pad, 300Hz-2kHz): Sustained minor chord voicings in the C3-C5 range with very long holds (3-6 seconds), creating a dreamy harmonic bed. Chords shift slowly following the same progression, with velocity 40-55 for a floating, ambient quality. Filter-sweep feel is achieved through gradual velocity changes. MELODY (Sawtooth Lead, 1-8kHz): Gentle arpeggiated patterns in C4-C6 range using chord tones on strong beats and scale passing tones on weak beats. Velocity stays soft (40-60) with moderate note density — enough to create gentle motion without urgency. The melody breathes with rests between phrases. ENERGY ARC: Segments (~7.5s each): 0.3→0.5→0.6→0.7→0.6→0.4. The piece opens sparse and quiet, builds gently to a warm peak around 22-30s, then gracefully fades. All tracks crescendo and decrescendo together. The chord progression cycles as: Cm(i) for 2 bars → Ab(bVI) for 1 bar → Bb(bVII) for 1 bar → repeat. At 61 BPM, one bar ≈ 3.93s. The 4-bar cycle ≈ 15.74s, giving roughly 3 full cycles in 45 seconds. All tracks change chords at the same moments, creating unity.

SLEEP MODE Electronic/House — Ultra-gentle, dark, warm, enveloping ambient wash. NO drums. Three tracks occupy distinct frequency bands to create a rich but sleepy sonic blanket.

FREQUENCY SEPARATION:
- BASS (Synth Bass, 20-300Hz, MIDI 33-45): Ultra-slow, quiet root notes with long sustains (4-8s). Provides a warm, dark foundation like a distant heartbeat. Plays chord roots only (A, D, F, G in low octaves). Velocity 25-40 — barely felt, more like a subsonic hum.
- CHORDS (Warm Pad, 300Hz-2kHz, MIDI 48-67): Lush sustained minor chord voicings (8-16s sustains). These are the harmonic body — slow-moving, overlapping pads that breathe gently. Velocity 30-45. Voicings use root-3rd-5th with occasional 7ths for warmth.
- MELODY (Sawtooth Lead used as soft high texture, 1kHz+, MIDI 64-79): Sparse, lonely high notes — one every 3-5 seconds. Like distant stars twinkling. Very low velocity (25-40). These are chord tones and gentle passing tones from A natural minor scale. No arpeggiation — just floating single notes.

HARMONIC UNITY: All tracks follow the same slow progression: Am → Dm → F → G → Am → Dm → Am (i → iv → bVI → bVII → i → iv → i). Chord changes happen every ~6-8 seconds, giving each chord time to breathe and settle.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments, ~7.5s each):
Seg1 (0-7.5s): 0.3 — Bass and pad fade in gently on Am. Melody silent or one note.
Seg2 (7.5-15s): 0.4 — Dm chord, melody enters with sparse notes. Slightly fuller.
Seg3 (15-22.5s): 0.5 — F chord, peak warmth. All three layers present but still whisper-quiet.
Seg4 (22.5-30s): 0.5 — G chord, sustained gentle peak. Melody slightly more active.
Seg5 (30-37.5s): 0.4 — Return to Am then Dm, energy receding. Melody thins.
Seg6 (37.5-45s): 0.3 — Final Am, everything fades to near-silence. Long held tones dissolving.

RHYTHMIC APPROACH: No rhythmic pulse. Everything floats freely with long overlapping sustains. This is ambient sleep music — time should feel suspended. Notes drift in and out like breathing.

FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Synth Bass 38) owns 20-300Hz with sub notes D1-D2 (MIDI 26-38), providing the 808-style foundation. Chords (Warm Pad 89) fill 300Hz-2kHz with dark minor voicings in C3-C5 (MIDI 48-72). Melody (Vibraphone 11) cuts through at 1-8kHz in C4-C6 (MIDI 60-79). Drums span full spectrum rhythmically with boom-bap kick/snare locking to bass hits.

HARMONIC UNITY: All tracks follow i (Dm) → iv (Gm) → bVI (Bb) → bVII (C) progression in 4-bar phrases (~8.35s per phrase). Bass plays roots D, G, Bb, C. Chords voice full minor/major triads. Melody targets chord tones on beats 1 and 3 with pentatonic passing tones on weak beats.

FREQUENCY SEPARATION:
- DRUMS (full spectrum, rhythmic): Son clave 3-2 pattern anchors everything. Kick on beats 1 & 3, conga accents on the "and" of 2 and 4, timbale/hihat fills the high end. This is the rhythmic backbone — every other instrument locks to the clave.
- BASS (20-300Hz, MIDI 33-48): Tumbao pattern — syncopated, anticipating beat 1 by an eighth note. Plays chord roots (A, G, F, E) with octave jumps and chromatic approach tones. Locks tightly with kick drum. Punchy short notes (0.2-0.4s).
- PIANO/MONTUNO (300Hz-2kHz, MIDI 48-67): Repetitive arpeggiated montuno figure — two-bar pattern that outlines each chord with 3rds, 5ths, 7ths. Syncopated, locked to clave. Fills the mid-range harmonic body. Velocity pulses with clave accents.
- FLUTE MELODY (1-8kHz, MIDI 64-84): Syncopated dance phrases, call-and-response structure. Plays chord tones (root, 3rd, 5th) on strong beats, uses scale passing tones (A harmonic minor / E phrygian dominant) on weak beats. Breathes between phrases — never clutters the montuno.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK:
- Bass anticipates beat 1 (plays on the "and" of 4), drums hit beat 1 — creates the classic tumbao push-pull.
- Montuno piano plays steady 8th-note arpeggios locked to clave accents.
- Flute melody plays BETWEEN piano montuno peaks — call (flute) and response (piano fill).
- All instruments change chords together: Am (bars 1-2) → G (bars 3-4) → F (bars 5-6) → E7 (bars 7-8), repeating.

Seg 4 (22.5-30s): 0.9 — Flute plays higher, more syncopation, drums busier.

Seg 6 (37.5-45s): 0.7 — Cool down, sparser melody, bass holds longer, drums simplify.

Long sustains (3-6 seconds) create the dreamy, floating lo-fi atmosphere. Volume at 0.45 — felt more than heard, providing harmonic bed beneath the Rhodes.

MELODY (Electric Piano/Rhodes, 1-8kHz, MIDI 65-79): The lead voice playing gentle jazz phrases — arpeggiated 7th chords, stepwise motion, and breathing space. Chord tones land on strong beats (1 and 3), with scale passing tones on weak beats. Slightly behind the beat feel achieved through gentle velocity swells. Volume at 0.6 — present but intimate.

ENERGY ARC: Segments 1-6 map across 45 seconds (~7.5s each). Energy starts sparse (0.3) with just bass and pad, builds melody in segment 2 (0.5), fills out in segments 3-4 (0.6-0.7 peak), then gently recedes in segments 5-6 (0.6→0.4). Note density and velocity both follow this arc. All tracks change chords simultaneously on the same beats, creating a unified band feel.

sequences.mixStrategy:
This lo-fi hip hop sleep arrangement is built around the progression ii7 → V7 → Imaj7 → vi7 cycling through Eb major/dorian harmony (Fm7 → Bb7 → Ebmaj7 → Cm7). Three tracks fill distinct frequency bands like a warm blanket of sound:

FREQUENCY SEPARATION:
- BASS (Electric Bass, 20-300Hz, MIDI 31-48): Anchors the harmony with deep root notes and gentle 5th walks. Sits in the sub-bass to bass range, providing gravitational warmth. Sparse — only root movements with long sustains (4-8s), occasionally walking to the next chord root via chromatic approach.
- CHORDS (Warm Pad, 300Hz-2kHz, MIDI 51-67): Sustained open-voiced 7th chords that bloom slowly. Each chord is a 4-note voicing (root-3rd-5th-7th) spread across the mid range. These pads are the harmonic body — they change together with bass on chord boundaries and sustain for 6-10 seconds, creating a pillowy warmth.
- MELODY (Rhodes EP, 1-8kHz, MIDI 63-79): Sparse, gentle Rhodes phrases that float above the pad. Plays chord tones on strong beats (root, 3rd, 5th, 7th of current chord) with occasional dorian scale passing tones. Notes are widely spaced (2-6s apart) with long sustains, mimicking a sleepy jazz musician gently touching keys.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: No drums in sleep mode. Bass provides slow rhythmic anchoring (whole notes and longer). Pad sustains continuously beneath. Rhodes melody enters with gentle phrases that breathe — 1-2 notes, then silence, then another phrase. Everything feels like it's floating.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments of ~7.5s each):
- 0-7.5s (0.3): Bass and pad enter softly. One or two melody notes. Establishing warmth.
- 7.5-15s (0.4): Melody begins gentle phrases. Slightly more presence.
- 15-22.5s (0.5): Peak gentleness — all three tracks most active (still very sparse). Fuller chord voicings.
- 22.5-30s (0.5): Sustained peak. Melody plays its most expressive (but still minimal) phrases.
- 30-37.5s (0.4): Beginning to recede. Fewer melody notes. Velocities drop.
- 37.5-45s (0.3): Fade to near-silence. Final sustained chord and single melody note lingering.

HARMONIC PLAN (Eb key):
- Bars 1-2 (0-10s): Fm7 (F-Ab-C-Eb) — ii7
- Bars 3-4 (10-20s): Bb7 (Bb-D-F-Ab) — V7
- Bars 5-6 (20-30s): Ebmaj7 (Eb-G-Bb-D) — Imaj7
- Bars 7-8 (30-37.5s): Cm7 (C-Eb-G-Bb) — vi7
- Bar 9 (37.5-41s): Fm7 — ii7
- Bar 10 (41-45s): Bb7 → resolution hint — V7

All tracks change chords SIMULTANEOUSLY at these boundaries, creating unified harmonic motion.

sequences.mixStrategy:
FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Electric Bass, GM 33) owns 20-300Hz with MIDI 31-43 (G1-G2), providing the heavy foundation with dotted quarter-note reggae feel on root-5th patterns. Chords (Organ, GM 16) fill 300Hz-2kHz with MIDI 60-72 offbeat skank voicings — completely silent on downbeats, hitting every upbeat 'and' to create the signature reggae pulse. Melody (Nylon Guitar, GM 24) sits in 1-5kHz with MIDI 60-76, playing simple repetitive vocal-style hooks that breathe between the skank hits. Drums (GM 0 ch10) provide the one-drop foundation: kick ONLY on beat 3, rim click on beats 2 and 4, hi-hat on 8th notes throughout.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: The bass locks tightly with the kick on beat 3 and provides weight on beat 1. The organ skank fills every upbeat ('and'), creating the classic reggae chop. The melody floats above, playing longer sustained phrases that move between chord tones on strong beats and scale tones on weak beats. The hi-hat provides the steady 8th-note pulse that glues everything together.

HARMONIC UNITY: All tracks follow the same I-IV progression (G-C) in G Mixolydian, changing together every 2 bars (6.4 seconds). The 6-segment energy arc (0.4→0.6→0.7→0.7→0.7→0.5) is achieved by: Segment 1 (0-7.5s) — sparse intro, bass+hats only entering; Segment 2 (7.5-15s) — full band enters with moderate velocity; Segments 3-5 (15-37.5s) — full energy plateau with consistent density for focus habituation; Segment 6 (37.5-45s) — gentle pullback, fewer melody notes, softer velocities.

FOCUS MODE DESIGN: Velocities stay in 45-75 range (except bass at 80-90 for weight and kick at 85). Note durations are 1-4 seconds for melody and bass. The repetitive I-IV progression and consistent rhythmic pattern create a steady, predictable texture that habituates quickly, perfect for focus.

This relaxation reggae arrangement uses three tracks occupying distinct frequency bands to create a warm, spacious island feel. BASS (Electric Bass, 20-300Hz): The foundation locks into a classic reggae dotted-quarter pattern, playing root notes and 5ths of each chord with long sustains. It sits deep in the E1-E3 range, providing warmth and weight. CHORDS (Organ, 300Hz-2kHz): The iconic reggae offbeat skank — silent on every downbeat, hitting on the 'and' of each beat. Voiced in the C4-C6 range, the organ chords provide harmonic body and the characteristic reggae rhythmic pulse. MELODY (Nylon Guitar, 1kHz-8kHz): A gentle, singable melody in the C4-C5 range that floats above the rhythm section. It plays chord tones on strong beats and uses passing scale tones on weak beats, with moderate sustains for a relaxed feel. RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Bass plays on downbeats with long sustains, organ hits on upbeats creating the offbeat skank, and melody weaves between them with longer phrases. This creates the classic reggae push-pull feel. ENERGY ARC: Segments 1(0-7.5s)=0.3 sparse entry, 2(7.5-15s)=0.5 building, 3(15-22.5s)=0.6 gentle peak, 4(22.5-30s)=0.7 fullest energy, 5(30-37.5s)=0.6 easing, 6(37.5-45s)=0.4 gentle fade. CHORD PROGRESSION: I(C) → V(G) → vi(Am) → IV(F), repeating every ~6.5 bars (~25.6s), with all tracks changing chords together. The quarter note = 0.984s, so half note = 1.968s, whole note = 3.936s. Upbeats fall at +0.492s from each beat.

This sleep reggae arrangement uses 3 tracks carefully separated by frequency to create a warm, enveloping bed of sound. The chord progression is I (G) → IV (C) → I (G) → V (D) → IV (C), repeating in long 10-second phrases. BASS (Electric Bass, 100-300Hz): Anchors everything with extremely slow, sustained root notes (G2, C2, D2) in the low register (MIDI 31-43). Notes are long (4-8s) with gentle velocity (30-45), providing a deep, warm foundation. Uses root-5th movement with whole notes and dotted halves. CHORDS (Organ, 300Hz-2kHz): Plays the classic reggae offbeat skank but slowed to a dreamlike pace. Chord voicings sit in MIDI 60-72 range. In sleep mode, these are extremely soft (vel 25-40), sustained pad-like washes on the upbeats with long sustains (4-8s), creating harmonic warmth without rhythmic urgency. Triads voiced as G-B-D, C-E-G, D-F#-A. MELODY (Nylon Guitar, 1-5kHz): Sparse, vocal-like phrases in MIDI 60-72 range. Plays only chord tones on strong beats with gentle passing tones. Very few notes (one every 2-4 seconds), long sustains (4-8s), creating a lullaby quality. Velocity 30-45. Energy arc: Segments 1-2 (0-15s) start very quiet with bass and sparse organ, melody enters gently. Segments 3-4 (15-30s) reach modest peak with all three tracks softly interacting. Segments 5-6 (30-45s) gradually fade, melody thins, bass sustains longer, organ drops out. All tracks change chords together at the same moments. NO drums per sleep mode. The result is a warm, dark, slow-motion reggae lullaby.

FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Electric Bass, GM 33) owns 20-300Hz with notes E1-E3 (MIDI 28-52), providing the rhythmic and harmonic foundation with driving eighth-note patterns that lock tightly with the kick drum. Chords (Warm Pad, GM 89) fill 300Hz-2kHz with sustained power chords (root+5th) in the E2-B4 range (MIDI 40-71), creating a thick harmonic bed underneath. Melody (Overdriven Guitar, GM 29) cuts through at 1-8kHz with pentatonic riffs in E4-E5 (MIDI 64-76), playing the lead voice with punchy rhythmic stabs and melodic phrases. Drums (GM 0 channel 10) provide full-spectrum rhythmic drive with kick on 1&3, snare on 2&4, hi-hat eighths, and crash accents at section boundaries.

HARMONIC PLAN: The progression is Em → D → C → D (i → bVII → bVI → bVII) for the verse segments, and Em → C → D → D (i → bVI → bVII → bVII) for the chorus/peak segments. All tracks change chords on the SAME beats. Each chord lasts 2 bars (4 beats = ~2.087s at 115 BPM).

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Bass plays driving eighth notes that align with kick drum hits on beats 1 and 3. Snare accents beats 2 and 4 for backbeat drive. Hi-hat plays steady eighths for momentum. Melody riffs land on chord tones on strong beats and use pentatonic passing tones on weak beats. The pad sustains underneath to glue everything together.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments of ~7.5s each): Seg1 (0-7.5s) 0.4 energy — sparse intro, bass and drums only, lower velocities. Seg2 (7.5-15s) 0.6 — chords enter, melody hints. Seg3 (15-22.5s) 0.8 — full band, melody riffs intensify. Seg4 (22.5-30s) 0.9 — peak verse energy, dense riffing. Seg5 (30-37.5s) 1.0 — climax, highest velocities, crash cymbals, most dense. Seg6 (37.5-45s) 0.7 — controlled descent, sparser melody, sustained chords fade.

sequences.mixStrategy:
This rock focus arrangement in Em uses a I-bVII-IV progression (Em-D-Am) cycling every 8 beats (6.4s). The energy arc builds from 0.4→0.6→0.7→0.7→0.7→0.5 across six ~7.5s segments.

FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Electric Bass, MIDI 28-47) anchors 20-300Hz with driving eighth-note root patterns that lock tightly with the kick drum. Chords (Warm Pad, MIDI 50-67) fill 300Hz-2kHz with sustained power chord voicings (root+5th) providing harmonic warmth. Melody (Overdriven Guitar, MIDI 60-76) occupies 1-8kHz with pentatonic riffs using moderate note lengths (1-3s) for focus-friendly predictability. Drums provide the rhythmic skeleton: kick on 1&3, snare on 2&4, hi-hat eighths throughout.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Bass and kick drum hit together on beats 1 and 3, creating a tight low-end pulse. Snare accents on 2&4 provide the backbeat. Melody phrases breathe between snare hits, landing chord tones on strong beats. The pad sustains underneath everything, gluing the harmony together.

ENERGY ARC: Segments 1 (0-7.5s) starts sparse with lower velocities. Segments 2-3 (7.5-22.5s) add density and velocity. Segments 4-5 (22.5-37.5s) maintain peak focus energy. Segment 6 (37.5-45s) pulls back for a gentle wind-down. All tracks follow the same Em-D-Am progression, changing chords together on the same beats throughout.

This relaxation rock arrangement in Am uses three tracks that occupy distinct frequency bands to create a warm, spacious, floating quality. The BASS (Electric Bass, GM 33) anchors the sub-bass and bass region (MIDI 33-48, ~55-130Hz), playing slow, sustained root notes and fifths that outline the chord progression Am → G → F → Am → G → F → Am. Notes are long (2-4s) with gentle velocity (40-60), providing a deep, pillowy foundation. The CHORDS (Warm Pad, GM 89) fill the low-mid to mid range (MIDI 52-67, ~300Hz-1.5kHz) with sustained power chord voicings (root+5th), each held for 3-6 seconds, creating a wide harmonic bed. Velocity is soft (35-55) to keep things dreamy and non-aggressive. The MELODY (Overdriven Guitar, GM 29) floats in the high-mid/presence range (MIDI 60-76, ~1-5kHz) with slow, lyrical pentatonic phrases. Notes are medium-long (1.5-4s) with gentle velocity (40-65), using lots of space between phrases to breathe. Energy arc: Segments 1 (0-7.5s) at 0.3 — sparse entry, bass and pad only with a single melody note; Segment 2 (7.5-15s) at 0.5 — melody enters with gentle phrases; Segment 3 (15-22.5s) at 0.6 — slightly fuller, more melodic movement; Segment 4 (22.5-30s) at 0.7 — peak warmth, all tracks most active; Segment 5 (30-37.5s) at 0.6 — gentle pullback; Segment 6 (37.5-45s) at 0.4 — fade to stillness. All tracks follow the same harmonic rhythm: Am for ~4 beats, G for ~4 beats, F for ~4 beats, cycling. No drums — pure floating rock meditation. Rhythmic interlock: bass moves on chord changes, pad sustains across, melody weaves between bass movements with chord tones on downbeats.

This sleep-mode rock arrangement strips the genre to its darkest, most enveloping essence — no drums, extremely sparse, long-sustaining tones that blur into a warm, dark blanket of sound.

FREQUENCY SEPARATION:
- BASS (Electric Bass, 20-300Hz, MIDI 28-43): Ultra-slow root notes with enormous sustains (8-16s). Provides the gravitational foundation in sub-bass and bass territory. Plays chord roots E1, D2, A1, forming the I→bVII→IV progression in glacial cycles. Velocity 25-40 for a felt-more-than-heard rumble.
- CHORDS (Warm Pad, 300Hz-2kHz, MIDI 48-67): Sustained power chord voicings (root+5th) that bloom slowly and overlap, creating a continuous harmonic bed. These fill the mid-range warmth zone. Velocity 30-45 for gentle, enveloping warmth. Each chord sustains 8-12s with slow crossfades.
- MELODY (Overdriven Guitar, 1kHz-8kHz, MIDI 60-76): Extremely sparse pentatonic fragments — single notes with long sustains (4-8s), like distant guitar harmonics echoing through a vast space. Only 1-2 notes per chord change. Velocity 25-45 for a ghostly, ethereal presence.

HARMONIC COHESION:
All three tracks follow the same slow chord progression: Em (I) → D (bVII) → Am (IV), cycling approximately every 15 seconds (12 beats at 48 BPM). The progression repeats 3 times across 45 seconds:
- Cycle 1 (0-15s): Em → D → Am — Energy 0.3, barely audible emergence
- Cycle 2 (15-30s): Em → D → Am — Energy 0.4-0.5, gentle peak of warmth
- Cycle 3 (30-45s): Em → D → Am — Energy 0.4→0.3, gradual dissolve into silence

RHYTHMIC APPROACH:
There is essentially NO rhythm — just overlapping sustains. Notes enter at slightly staggered times to create a breathing, organic feel. Bass enters first on each chord, pad blooms next, melody floats in last. This staggering creates gentle movement without any pulse. The overall effect is like hearing a rock band from very far away, through walls, as you fall asleep — just warm harmonic ghosts.
